summary_one_line: An iTXt chunk whose keyword field is either empty (first byte NUL) or runs more than 79 bytes before the first NUL terminator.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A PNG input that reaches an iTXt chunk in which the keyword prefix scan computes prefix_length > 79 OR prefix_length < 1. Concretely: an iTXt chunk payload whose first byte is 0x00 (empty keyword) or whose first 80+ bytes contain no 0x00 terminator (over-long keyword).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Naive uses pure random byte mutation (byte/word/dword random/interesting/flip/inc, bytes insert/delete). Random byte placement makes it easy to either set the first iTXt payload byte to 0 (length=0) or to fill 80+ bytes of the iTXt payload with non-zero bytes (length>79). Naive resolves the branch in 8/10 trials.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes input bytes with operands seen at intercepted CMPs (PNG chunk-type magic numbers like 'IHDR'/'iTXt', length fields, CRC operands). These substitutions push the input toward well-formed PNG keyword structures (printable ASCII followed by a NUL within 1-79 bytes), keeping prefix_length inside the valid 1..79 range. Cmplog reaches png_handle_iTXt MUCH more often than naive (HIT-COUNT DIVERGENCE shows ~10-20x more invocations) yet resolves the branch in only 1/10 trials — the I2S bias steers AROUND the malformed-keyword condition rather than toward it.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Pattern-B: I2SRandReplace (cmplog's distinguishing technique vs naive) HURTS here. The branch operand prefix_length is a runtime-computed loop-count, not a literal that any CMP records, so I2S has no direct lever to set it. Meanwhile the CMPs I2S DOES see (chunk magic 'IHDR'/'iTXt', CRC, fixed-format header fields) are constraints whose satisfaction produces well-formed keywords, which fall in the [1,79] range the branch checks AGAINST. Naive's unbiased random mutation explores the malformed boundary cases more readily.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a compound length-range check on a runtime-computed keyword length, not a direct equality CMP on input bytes.
prefix_length is computed by a NUL-scan loop over the iTXt chunk buffer; it is not an input field that any CMP intercepts as a literal operand.
Naive resolves this branch 8/10 trials while cmplog resolves only 1/10 — the I2S-augmented fuzzer is strictly worse here.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive                            8        2          0  winner (I2S vs cmplog)
cmplog                           1        9          0  loser (I2S vs naive)

evidence_trail.claim: The naive>cmplog separation is statistically strong at subject level (p_AUC=0.0002, p_final=0.0002).

Cmplog (L) hits the PNG-parsing call chain dramatically more often than naive (W) — e.g. png_get_uint_31 fires 214 times for L vs 14 for W, png_check_chunk_name 192 vs 12 — yet still fails to reach the resolved side. This means cmplog is spending its budget re-parsing well-formed PNGs rather than producing the malformed iTXt keyword.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
W hits    L hits  function  (file:start-end)
      14       214  OSS_FUZZ_png_get_uint_31  (/src/libpng/pngrutil.c:23-30)
      17       205  OSS_FUZZ_png_crc_read  (/src/libpng/pngrutil.c:197-203)
      12       194  OSS_FUZZ_png_read_chunk_header  (/src/libpng/pngrutil.c:157-192)
      12       192  OSS_FUZZ_png_check_chunk_name  (/src/libpng/pngrutil.c:3136-3151)

evidence_trail.claim: At the blocker itself, cmplog (L) DOES occasionally take the TRUE side (3/12) but more often takes FALSE (9/12 and 12/12 across two branch records), while naive (W) shows T=1/3 and T=1/4 — both sparse, but naive arrives with less of the auxiliary FALSE-side noise. Cmplog explores the FALSE-side downstream code (lines 2780-2850 marked [L]) extensively, which is precisely the well-formed-keyword path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
d=1   L2767  T=1 F=2  T=3 F=9  if (prefix_length > 79 || prefix_length < 1)  <-- BLOCKER
  d=1   L2767  T=1 F=3  T=0 F=12  if (prefix_length > 79 || prefix_length < 1)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: I2SRandReplace is available to cmplog and substitutes intercepted CMP operands (chunk magic, length fields, CRC operands) into the input, biasing toward well-formed PNG structures.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: Naive's mutator stack is purely structure-agnostic random byte/word/dword/bits transforms with no CMP introspection, so its output distribution over byte sequences is unbiased relative to PNG well-formedness.

The rare cmplog winner's lineage does contain I2S-floor dash rows, confirming I2S DID fire — but it produced a win only 1/10 times because most I2S firings push the input toward well-formed PNG and away from the malformed-keyword condition.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Seed 1 (id=8d7db84a6b1ec8bc, size=8759 bytes, fuzzer=cmplog, trial=4, discovered_at=7348s, mutation_op=BytesDeleteMutator,ByteRandMutator,WordInterestingMutator,ByteInterestingMutator,ByteInterestingMutator,TokenInsert,ByteAddMutator)

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 7424 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 4 --seed 8d7db84a6b1ec8bc` and observed I2S-floor dash rows at depths 7 and 8 of the cmplog winner's ancestor chain (output: 'I2S-floor signal: 2 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 7, 8'). I2SRandReplace IS the distinguishing technique at this branch — but this is a Pattern-B (technique-HURTS) case: cmplog is the LOSER. I2S fires for cmplog (verified), but its substitutions push the input toward well-formed PNG keyword structures (length 1-79) and AWAY from the malformed-keyword condition (length 0 or >79) that the gate requires. Naive, lacking I2S bias, finds the malformed condition through unbiased random mutation. The named technique (I2SRandReplace) is the same as the agreed mechanism in mechanism_attribution; the polarity (helps vs hurts) is captured in the hypothesis text, not in the technique label.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the gate operand is a length scanned from a NUL-terminated input field embedded behind a PNG-style magic-number prelude should show naive > cmplog when the gate boundary lies OUTSIDE the range I2S substitutions would naturally produce (length < 1 or > 79). If, instead, a synthetic with this exact shape shows cmplog >= naive (or no separation), the 'I2S bias steers AWAY from malformed boundary' mechanism is refuted.

weakest_evidence_point: The claim that I2S substitutions actively bias cmplog toward valid keyword lengths (rather than just spending budget on other parsing work) is inferential — we observe the HIT-COUNT divergence and infer the mechanism, but we did not directly trace which I2S substitutions wrote which bytes into the keyword field of any specific cmplog input.
